{"description":"Backend rate limiting for oracle queries at /api/query.

## Limit rules
- Anonymous: 3 queries/day. Tracked client-side in localStorage as { count: number, date: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }. Client enforces before hitting API; API trusts client for anonymous (no user_id). Reset at midnight UTC — date comparison against today.
- Casual: 15/day for first 7 days post-signup (signup_date stored on users row), then 5/day. Tracked server-side in oracle_queries table: { user_id, date, count }. Upsert on each query.
- Spike: 30/day. Same tracking.

## API response shape
Every successful query response includes rate limit state:
  {
    answer: string,
    ...existing fields...,
    rate_limit: {
      remaining: number,
      resets_at: string,  // ISO8601, always midnight UTC of current day
      tier: 'anon' | 'casual' | 'spike'
    }
  }

On limit exceeded, return 429 with:
  { error: 'rate_limit_exceeded', rate_limit: { remaining: 0, resets_at: string, tier } }

## Anonymous query pool is global per-browser-day
The landing page input at / routes to /chat?q=... which auto-submits on /chat. All oracle queries go through /chat → /api/query, so the anonymous count is naturally unified. No cross-page coordination needed.

## DB schema addition
oracle_queries table:
  user_id uuid references auth.users
  date date
  count integer default 1
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, date)

users table addition:
  signup_date date not null default current_date  (set on first OAuth login upsert)

## Acceptance
Anonymous: 3/day via localStorage, 429 on 4th, resets_at in response. Casual: correct tier-aware limit with 7-day bonus. Spike: 30/day. All 429s include resets_at."}

{"description":"Full /chat UI. Three user states: anonymous, casual, spike. Rate limit state drives most of the conditional UI.

---

## PHILOSOPHY
The rate limit is a conversion funnel, not a punishment. Every state has a clear next action. The oracle response always renders completely before any limit prompt — value first, ask second. All prompts are inline in the conversation. No modals. No redirects. No dead ends.

---

## ANONYMOUS STATE (no account)

### Query counter — progressive disclosure
- Before query 1 / empty state: NO counter visible. Clean slate, no anxiety.
- After query 1 response renders: subtle 'X of 3 free queries used' appears in the meta footer area of that response (same text-xs text-ash style as confidence/window_days). Low prominence. Informational only.
- After query 2 response renders: '2 of 3 free queries used' in same position, but text-gold. The query input still works normally.
- After query 3 response renders: input is disabled. The inline auth prompt card appears in the conversation flow below the response (see below).

### Input bar — anonymous at limit
- Input field: visible but disabled (opacity-50)
- No counter text above input — the inline auth card in the conversation is the primary communication
- Input placeholder changes to: 'Sign in to keep going…'

### Inline auth prompt card (appears after 3rd response)
This is the most important UI element in the anonymous flow. It must feel like a companion message, not a paywall.

Styling: same visual position as an oracle message (left-aligned, full width of conversation). NOT the spark-blue oracle-border. Use a copper/flame gradient left border instead — visually distinct from oracle responses, still in the design language.

  border-left: 2px solid; 
  border-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, #B87333, #D4552A) 1;
  (or closest Tailwind equivalent using copper/flame tokens)

Content:
  ┌─ copper-flame left border ──────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                          │
  │  You've got good questions.                              │
  │  Create a free account to keep going — no credit        │
  │  card, ever.                                             │
  │                                                          │
  │  [G  Sign in with Google]   (primary button, full width) │
  │                                                          │
  │  or wait · resets in 09:14:33   (text-xs text-ash,      │
  │                                  live HH:MM:SS ticker)  │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

- NO dismiss button. The prompt is persistent. Scroll up and the input is still disabled.
- 'You've got good questions.' — affirmative framing. The oracle is impressed.
- 'or wait · resets in HH:MM:SS' — live countdown, same midnight UTC anchor. Gives users who resist signup a real alternative. Builds goodwill. Also makes signup feel easier by comparison.

### OAuth flow — context preservation
Use Supabase signInWithOAuth with popup: true. The main window keeps React state (conversation) alive.
On popup auth success, onAuthStateChange fires in the main window → upgrade session → remove auth card → re-enable input → user continues exactly where they left off.
Popup blocked fallback: serialize messages to localStorage before redirect, restore on return to /chat.

---

## CASUAL STATE (free account, authenticated)

### Query counter — always visible, color-coded
Lives above the input bar, right-aligned. Shows throughout the session.

States:
- ≥3 remaining: 'X queries left today'  →  text-xs text-ash  (low prominence)
- 2 remaining:  '2 queries left today'  →  text-xs text-gold  (amber warning)
- 1 remaining:  '1 query left today · Go Spike →'  →  text-xs text-flame  (CTA appears early, at 1 remaining, before they hit the wall)
- 0 remaining:  counter replaced with live countdown (see below)

### Input bar — Casual at limit
- Input: visible but disabled (opacity-50)
- Above input (where counter was): '⏱ Resets in 03:42:17 · Go Spike →'
  - ⏱ in text-ash
  - HH:MM:SS in text-ink, monospace font (tabular-nums), live ticker updating every second
  - ' · ' separator
  - 'Go Spike →' in text-spark, hover:underline — links to /pricing or opens upgrade modal

The ticking clock is the conversion mechanic. It makes the alternative (waiting 3+ hours) concrete and slightly tedious without being hostile. Users mid-research session who hit the wall will convert here.

### No inline card
Casual limit is communicated entirely via the counter/countdown in the input bar. No conversation-interrupting card. They're authenticated, they know the rules, the counter has been counting down all session.

---

## SPIKE STATE (paid)

### Query counter
'X / 30 today' — text-xs text-ash, right-aligned above input. Informational only.
Never disabled. Never countdown. Never a CTA. Just a number.

---

## EMPTY STATE (no messages yet, all tiers)
Centered suggested prompts with radial-gradient glow (already implemented).
Counter for Casual/Spike shows above input.
Anonymous: no counter on empty state.

---

## ONBOARDING BONUS (Casual, days 1-7)
Counter shows '15 queries left today' during bonus period. No special UI callout — just the higher number. On day 8 when it drops to 5, no notification — they'll see '5 queries left today' on first load.

---

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All three tiers render correct counter states
- [ ] Anonymous counter appears only after query 1 (not before)
- [ ] Inline auth card appears after query 3 response with copper-flame border, correct copy, live countdown
- [ ] OAuth popup preserves conversation context
- [ ] Casual countdown is live HH:MM:SS updating every second
- [ ] 'Go Spike →' CTA appears at 1 remaining (Casual) and at limit (Casual)
- [ ] Spike sees no warning states, no countdown
- [ ] Input disabled correctly at limit for both anonymous and casual"}

{"description":"Stripe subscription integration. Supabase is the fast-path source of truth for tier; Stripe webhooks keep it in sync. Never query Stripe at request time — read users.tier from Supabase.

---

## DATA MODEL ADDITIONS

Add to public.users table (migration required):
  stripe_customer_id        text unique,         -- null until first checkout attempt
  stripe_subscription_id    text unique,         -- null until active subscription
  stripe_subscription_status text,               -- 'active' | 'past_due' | 'canceled' | 'trialing' | null
  spike_started_at          timestamptz          -- set on first upgrade, never cleared

signup_date date should already exist from toy.1. Confirm before migrating.

---

## STRIPE PRODUCTS

Two prices, one product ('Firemind Spike'):
  STRIPE_PRICE_MONTHLY = price_xxx  (.99/month)
  STRIPE_PRICE_ANNUAL  = price_xxx  (.88/year — ~.99/mo, 20% off)
Create in Stripe dashboard, store IDs as env vars. Reference by env var in code, never hardcode.

---

## SERVER-SIDE API ROUTES

### POST /api/stripe/checkout
- Requires auth (anonymous users must sign up first — return 401 with message 'create_account_first')
- Body: { price: 'monthly' | 'annual' }
- Create or retrieve Stripe customer:
    If users.stripe_customer_id is null: stripe.customers.create({ email, metadata: { supabase_user_id } })
    Update users.stripe_customer_id
- Create checkout session:
    mode: 'subscription'
    customer: stripe_customer_id
    line_items: [{ price: STRIPE_PRICE_MONTHLY or ANNUAL, quantity: 1 }]
    success_url: /settings?upgrade=success
    cancel_url: /pricing
    allow_promotion_codes: true
- Return { url: session.url }
- Client redirects to Stripe's hosted checkout — do NOT build a custom card form

### POST /api/stripe/portal
- Requires auth + stripe_customer_id (user must have gone through checkout at least once)
- Create billing portal session: stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({ customer: stripe_customer_id, return_url: /settings })
- Return { url: portalSession.url }
- Client redirects — Stripe portal handles: cancel, change payment method, change plan (monthly ↔ annual), view invoices

### POST /api/webhooks/stripe
- Raw body required — disable Next.js body parser on this route:
    export const config = { api: { bodyParser: false } }  (Pages router)
    or: read request.arrayBuffer() and pass raw buffer to constructEvent (App router)
- Verify signature: stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET) — return 400 on failure
- Handle events:

  checkout.session.completed:
    → users SET tier='spike', stripe_subscription_id=session.subscription, stripe_subscription_status='active', spike_started_at=now() WHERE stripe_customer_id=session.customer

  customer.subscription.updated:
    → users SET stripe_subscription_status=subscription.status WHERE stripe_subscription_id=subscription.id
    → if status='active': also SET tier='spike'
    → if status='canceled': also SET tier='casual'

  customer.subscription.deleted:
    → users SET tier='casual', stripe_subscription_status='canceled', stripe_subscription_id=null WHERE stripe_subscription_id=subscription.id

  invoice.payment_failed:
    → if event.data.object.next_payment_attempt is not null:
        users SET stripe_subscription_status='past_due'  (keep tier='spike' — grace period, Stripe is retrying)
    → if next_payment_attempt is null (all retries exhausted):
        users SET tier='casual', stripe_subscription_status='canceled'

  invoice.payment_succeeded:
    → users SET tier='spike', stripe_subscription_status='active' WHERE stripe_customer_id=invoice.customer

  Always return 200 to Stripe even for unhandled event types (prevents retries).

---

## GRACE PERIOD BEHAVIOUR

{"id":"mindsculptor-toy.2","title":"Lemon Squeezy subscription integration — Casual/Spike tiers","description":"Lemon Squeezy subscription integration. Lemon Squeezy is the merchant of record — they handle all VAT, global sales tax, and year-end tax reporting. You never touch tax infrastructure.

Supabase is the fast-path source of truth for tier; Lemon Squeezy webhooks keep it in sync. Never query Lemon Squeezy at request time — read users.tier from Supabase.

---

WHY LEMON SQUEEZY OVER STRIPE
Lemon Squeezy is merchant of record: they collect and remit VAT/GST globally, handle US sales tax by state, and issue a single 1099 at year end. Stripe leaves all of that to you. For a solo indie product, this is the correct trade.

---

DATA MODEL ADDITIONS (migration required):
  lemon_customer_id         text unique  -- null until first checkout
  lemon_subscription_id     text unique  -- null until active subscription
  lemon_subscription_status text         -- 'active' | 'past_due' | 'cancelled' | 'expired' | null
  spike_started_at          timestamptz  -- set on first upgrade, never cleared

signup_date date should already exist from toy.1. Confirm before migrating.

---

LEMON SQUEEZY PRODUCTS

Two variants on one product (Firemind Spike):
  LS_VARIANT_MONTHLY  ($4.99/month)
  LS_VARIANT_ANNUAL   ($47.88/year ~$3.99/mo, 20% off)
Create in LS dashboard, store variant IDs as env vars.

---

API ROUTES

POST /api/ls/checkout
- Auth required (anonymous gets 401 with error: 'create_account_first')
- Body: { variant: 'monthly' | 'annual' }
- Use @lemonsqueezy/lemonsqueezy.js SDK
- createCheckout(storeId, variantId, { checkoutData: { email, custom: { supabase_user_id } }, productOptions: { redirectUrl: /settings?upgrade=success } })
- Return { url: checkout.data.attributes.url }
- Client redirects to LS hosted checkout — never build a custom card form

POST /api/ls/portal
- Auth required + lemon_subscription_id
- Redirect to LS customer portal (handles cancel, payment method, invoices)

POST /api/webhooks/lemonsqueezy
- Verify HMAC-SHA256 signature: X-Signature header vs createHmac('sha256', LS_WEBHOOK_SECRET).update(rawBody).digest('hex')
- Raw body required — disable body parser on this route
- Event name in body.meta.event_name

  subscription_created:
    users SET tier='spike', lemon_subscription_id, lemon_customer_id, lemon_subscription_status='active', spike_started_at=now()
    WHERE id = body.meta.custom_data.supabase_user_id

  subscription_updated:
    users SET lemon_subscription_status=status WHERE lemon_subscription_id=body.data.id
    if active: also SET tier='spike'
    if cancelled or expired: also SET tier='casual'

  subscription_cancelled:
    users SET lemon_subscription_status='cancelled' (keep tier='spike' until period end — LS fires subscription_expired at actual expiry)

  subscription_expired:
    users SET tier='casual', lemon_subscription_status='expired', lemon_subscription_id=null

  subscription_payment_failed:
    users SET lemon_subscription_status='past_due' (keep tier='spike' — grace period, LS retries)

  subscription_payment_success:
    users SET tier='spike', lemon_subscription_status='active'

  Always return 200 for unhandled event types.

---

GRACE PERIOD
LS retries failed payments. During retries status='past_due', tier stays 'spike'. Drops to casual only on subscription_expired. Never punish users for temporary card issues.

---

POST-CHECKOUT UX
/settings?upgrade=success:
1. Show 'Activating your Spike subscription...' + spinner
2. Supabase Realtime subscription on users row for current user_id
3. On tier flip to 'spike': show 'You are now on Spike.' success state
4. Timeout at 10s: 'Taking longer than expected — refresh the page'
Do NOT poll. Realtime only.

---

ANONYMOUS + GO SPIKE
Must have account first. Flow: Go Spike -> if anonymous -> inline auth prompt -> after OAuth -> /pricing -> checkout.

---

ENV VARS
  LEMONSQUEEZY_API_KEY
  LS_WEBHOOK_SECRET
  LS_STORE_ID
  LS_VARIANT_MONTHLY
  LS_VARIANT_ANNUAL

{"description":"Public SSR page, no auth required. Crawlable. Data freshness: revalidate every 3600s (ISR) — scraper runs every 12h so 1hr cache is a good balance of performance vs staleness.

URL: /data/[format] where format = 'modern' | 'standard'
URL params: ?range=7d|30d|90d (default: 30d). Range drives which snapshot windows are included. Range is a URL param (not client state) so pages are shareable and crawlable at each range.

/data alone redirects to /data/modern.

---

DATA QUERIES (run in Server Component, direct Supabase calls — no API route needed for SSR pages)

1. Meta share + top movers:
   SELECT
     ms.meta_share, ms.trend_delta, ms.top8_count, ms.total_entries,
     ms.sample_size, ms.confidence, ms.window_end,
     a.id, a.name, a.tier
   FROM metagame_snapshots ms
   JOIN archetypes a ON a.id = ms.archetype_id
   WHERE ms.format = [format]
     AND ms.window_end >= now() - interval '[range]'
   ORDER BY ms.window_end DESC, ms.meta_share DESC

   Take the most recent window_end as "current snapshot". Use trend_delta for movers.

2. Trend lines (multi-window history):
   Same query without the window_end filter — pull all snapshots for the format,
   grouped by archetype. Take only top 8 archetypes by current meta_share to keep
   the chart readable. Order by window_end ascending for chart x-axis.

3. Format header stats (total decks analyzed, date range, confidence):
   Derived from the snapshot rows — max(sample_size), max(confidence), min/max(window_end).

All three queries run in parallel via Promise.all.

---

COMPONENTS

MetaShareBar:
- Recharts HorizontalBarChart (BarChart with layout='vertical')
- Y axis: archetype name (links to /data/[format]/[archetype_id])
- X axis: meta_share %
- Bar fill: spark (#4F8EF7) at 70% opacity, full opacity on hover
- Tier badge (S/A/B/C) left of name, color-coded: S=spark, A=gold, B=copper, C=ash
- Sort toggle (client-side only, no refetch): sort by meta share (default) or win rate
  Win rate sort: disabled with tooltip 'Win rate requires match data' until matches table has rows
- Time range tabs: 7d / 30d / 90d — these are links that change ?range= param, not client state

TopMoversWidget:
- NOT a chart — a two-column ranked list: Gainers | Losers
- Derived from trend_delta on the current snapshot
- Show top 3 gainers (trend_delta > 0, sorted desc) and top 3 losers (trend_delta < 0, sorted asc)
- Format: 'Boros Energy +3.8%' in text-spark, 'Rhinos -2.1%' in text-flame
- Empty state: 'Trend data requires multiple snapshot windows — check back soon'
  (shown when all trend_delta values are null or 0, which is the case with a single snapshot window)

TrendLines:
- Recharts LineChart
- X axis: window_end dates
- Y axis: meta_share %
- One Line per top 8 archetype, each a different color (spark palette with opacity variants)
- Hoverable legend, archetype names link to detail pages
- Minimum viable: need >= 3 data points per archetype for a meaningful line
  Empty state: 'Not enough history yet — trend lines appear after multiple weeks of data'

ConfidenceBadge:
- Reuse the same LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/VERY HIGH treatment from the oracle (text-flame/gold/spark)
- Shown in the page header: 'Based on [sample_size] decks · [confidence]'
- Tooltip: 'Confidence reflects sample size and recency of tournament data'

OracleSummaryCard (bottom of page):
- Static component — no oracle query on page load (too expensive for SSR)
- Shows: '⚡ Ask the Firemind about [format]' with a pre-filled query link to /chat?q=...
- Example: '?q=What%27s+dominating+Modern+right+now%3F'
- CTA button links to /chat — uses a query quota when clicked

---

LOADING STATES (Suspense boundaries per section)

Each section (MetaShareBar, TopMovers, TrendLines) is wrapped in its own Suspense with a skeleton:
- MetaShareBar skeleton: 8 gray rounded bars of varying width, staggered opacity
- TopMovers skeleton: 6 gray text lines in two columns
- TrendLines skeleton: a flat gray area where the chart will be

Use Tailwind animate-pulse on all skeleton elements.
Do NOT show a full-page spinner — sections load independently.

---

EMPTY / NO DATA STATES

If metagame_snapshots has no rows for this format:
  Show full-page empty state: 'No data yet for [format] — check back after the next scrape runs'
  with a timestamp of when the next scrape is expected (hardcode '12 hours' for now)

If snapshots exist but trend_delta is all null (single window):
  TopMovers: 'Trend data appears after multiple events — check back soon'
  TrendLines: 'Not enough history yet'
  MetaShareBar: renders normally (single window is enough for a bar chart)

---

PAGE HEADER
Format name + format selector toggle (Modern / Standard tabs)
'Last updated: [computed_at of most recent snapshot]'
ConfidenceBadge

---

SEO
- <title>: 'Modern Metagame — Firemind' (server-rendered)
- <meta description>: 'Top Modern decks this week: [top 3 archetype names] leading the field.'
- OG image: format name + top 3 archetypes + meta share % (toy.11)
- All archetype names in the bar chart are real HTML text — crawlable

---

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] /data redirects to /data/modern
- [ ] ?range param drives snapshot window, default 30d, all three values work
- [ ] MetaShareBar renders with real data, archetype names link to detail pages
- [ ] TopMovers shows empty state gracefully when trend_delta is null
- [ ] TrendLines shows empty state gracefully when < 3 data points
- [ ] Each section has independent Suspense skeleton
- [ ] ConfidenceBadge matches oracle visual treatment
- [ ] Page is fully SSR, revalidates every hour
- [ ] No auth required, no client-side gating

{"description":"Public SSR page, no auth required. The main SEO surface — one page per archetype per format. Crawlable. revalidate: 3600.

URL: /data/[format]/[archetypeId] where archetypeId is the archetype slug (e.g. 'izzet-murktide-modern')
generateStaticParams: pre-render all known archetypes at build time using SELECT id, format FROM archetypes.
Unknown archetype IDs return 404.

---

DATA QUERIES (parallel, Server Component)

1. Archetype header:
   SELECT id, name, format, tier, description, key_cards
   FROM archetypes WHERE id = [archetypeId]
   → 404 if not found

2. Latest snapshot stats (meta share, win rate, trend):
   SELECT meta_share, trend_delta, top8_count, total_entries, sample_size, confidence, window_end
   FROM metagame_snapshots
   WHERE archetype_id = [archetypeId]
   ORDER BY window_end DESC LIMIT 1

3. Historical snapshots for charts:
   SELECT meta_share, window_end
   FROM metagame_snapshots
   WHERE archetype_id = [archetypeId]
   ORDER BY window_end ASC
   -- Used for ShareOverTime chart

4. Win rate over time (from matches table):
   For each snapshot window, compute:
     wins WHERE (deck_a_id in archetype decks AND winner = player_a)
              OR (deck_b_id in archetype decks AND winner = player_b)
     total = wins + losses (excluding byes and draws)
     win_rate = wins / total
   
   IMPORTANT: matches table is likely empty right now (just created). This query
   should return null gracefully — the win rate section shows a pending state, not an error.
   Check: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches — if 0, skip this query entirely and show pending state.

5. Recent results (last 10 top-placing decks for this archetype):
   SELECT d.pilot, d.placement, d.record, t.name, t.date, t.tier, t.source_url
   FROM decks d
   JOIN tournaments t ON t.id = d.tournament_id
   JOIN deck_archetypes da ON da.deck_id = d.id
   WHERE da.archetype_id = [archetypeId]
     AND d.placement IS NOT NULL
   ORDER BY t.date DESC, d.placement ASC
   LIMIT 10

All queries run in parallel. Archetype header is fetched first (for 404 check), rest in parallel.

---

COMPONENTS

ArchetypeHeader:
- Archetype name (h1)
- Format badge (Modern / Standard pill)
- Tier badge (S/A/B/C) — color same as toy.9
- Meta share % (from latest snapshot) — large, prominent
- Trend arrow from trend_delta: ↑ text-spark, ↓ text-flame, → text-ash
- 'Based on [sample_size] decks · [confidence]' — same ConfidenceBadge as toy.9
- Win rate % if available, 'Win rate pending' (text-ash, italic) if matches table empty

ShareOverTime:
- Recharts AreaChart (single series looks better as area than line)
- X axis: window_end dates
- Y axis: meta_share %
- Fill: spark blue at 20% opacity, stroke at full opacity
- Time range toggle: 30d / 90d / all — client-side filter of already-fetched data (no refetch)
- Minimum data: >= 3 points. Empty state: 'Not enough history yet'
- GOTCHA: with only 4 tournaments currently in DB, this chart will likely show 1-2 points.
  Show gracefully: render the points as dots on the area chart, no connecting line if < 3 points.
  Display note: 'More data accumulates with each event — trend improves over time'

WinRateOverTime:
- Recharts LineChart, single series
- Same time range toggle as ShareOverTime (shared state)
- If matches table is empty (detected at data fetch time, passed as prop):
  Show placeholder panel: '⏳ Win rate data will appear as match records are ingested'
  styled like a chart skeleton but with a text message — NOT an error, just pending
- When data exists: win_rate % over time, stroke text-gold (#C9A050)

RecentResults:
- Plain table, no chart library needed
- Columns: Placement | Event | Tier | Pilot | Date | Record (if available)
- Placement: 1st gets text-gold, top 4 gets text-spark, rest text-ash
- Event tier badge: Challenge / Preliminary / RCQ — small pill
- Pilot name is plain text (no link — MTGO pilot pages are ephemeral)
- Source link: external link icon on event name linking to tournaments.source_url
- Empty state: 'No results recorded yet for this archetype'
- This section works with current data — most likely to have real content on first ship

OracleCTAs:
Always shown. Three actions, each clearly labeled with tier requirement:
  1. '⚡ Ask the Firemind about [archetype name]' — free, links to /chat?q=... with pre-filled query
     Pre-filled query example: 'Tell me about Izzet Murktide in Modern — is it worth playing right now?'
  2. 'Generate sample deck list' — Spike only. For now (no gating yet): show as active,
     links to /chat?q=Generate a sample 75 for [archetype] in [format]
     NOTE: when gating is built (toy.3), this becomes a soft gate with upgrade prompt for Casual
  3. 'Sideboard plan vs the field' — same as above, Spike-only post-gating

AlertBell (🔔):
- 'Alert me when this archetype top 8s'
- For now (no auth/gating): clicking opens the auth modal (Sign in to set alerts)
- Post-toy.1: Casual gets 1 alert, Spike gets unlimited

---

LOADING STATES

Each section in its own Suspense boundary:
- ArchetypeHeader skeleton: gray pill badges + two large gray stat blocks
- ShareOverTime skeleton: gray area chart shape (SVG rect with rounded top)
- WinRateOverTime skeleton: gray line chart shape or pending state if no match data
- RecentResults skeleton: 5 gray table rows, alternating opacity

---

EMPTY / ERROR STATES

- Archetype not found: full-page 404 with 'Archetype not found — it may not have been labeled yet'
  + link back to /data/[format]
- No snapshot data: header shows archetype info, charts show 'No data yet' state
- No recent results: 'No results recorded yet' — not an error
- Win rate empty: pending state (not error) — matches table is new

---

SEO
- <title>: '[Archetype Name] — [Format] Metagame — Firemind'
- <meta description>: '[Archetype Name] is a [tier] deck in [format] with [meta_share]% meta share. See recent results, trends, and oracle analysis.'
- OG image: archetype name, format, meta%, trend arrow (toy.11)
- All content real HTML — crawlable

---

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Correct archetype data loads for known IDs, 404 for unknown
- [ ] ArchetypeHeader shows all available stats, win rate shows pending state when matches empty
- [ ] ShareOverTime handles < 3 data points gracefully (shows dots + note)
- [ ] WinRateOverTime shows pending panel (not error) when matches table empty
- [ ] RecentResults table renders with real placement/event data
- [ ] OracleCTAs all link correctly with pre-filled queries
- [ ] Each section has independent Suspense skeleton
- [ ] revalidate: 3600 set at page level
- [ ] generateStaticParams pre-renders all known archetypes
"}
